title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 August 2024.

items.title: **Yakuza Kiwami** coming to Switch on October 24
bullets: Yakuza Kiwami first launched for PlayStation 4 and PlayStation 3 on January 21, 2016 in Japan, followed by PlayStation 4 on August 29, 2017 worldwide, PC via Steam on February 19, 2019, Xbox One on April 21, 2020, and Luna on December 22, 2022
Will be available via Nintendo eShop for $19.99 on October 24th

items.title: **Blue Protocol** will be discontinued on January 18th, 2025 in Japan and will no longer release worldwide
bullets: Players cite the disappointing end-game loop and loop monetization options as the primary reasons for the cancelled launch.

items.title: **Balatro**'s free collaboration update adds The Witcher, Vampire Survivors, Among Us, and more
bullets: Available now

Balatro's Friends of Jimbo update, as it's officially known, is available now and gives player the option to swap out the game's usual Kings, Queens, and Jacks with characters from The Witcher, Dave the Diver, Among Us and Vampire Survivors

items.title: **PowerWash Simulator** heading to **Shrek**'s fairytale world this autumn
bullets: Developer FuturLab's Shrek Special Pack promises five new "fairytale" maps inspired by the Dreamworks' currently four-strong animated movie series, with levels revealed so far including Shrek's Swamp and the Dragon's Lair.
Coming "this autumn" to all platforms. No price given yet.

items.title: **Pokémon Go** finally confirms its gigantic Dynamax feature
bullets: Gen 8 Starter Pokémon that debuted in Pokémon Sword and Shield will join Pokémon Go at the beginning of the next season. This includes Grass-type monkey Grookey, Fire-type rabbit Scorbunny, Water-type frog thing Sobble, plus Dragon-type - Dreepy and the Stonehenge-esque Stonjourner (which, excitingly for UK players, will be a regional exclusive).
Already-released Gen 8 Pokémon Skwovet and Wooloo will become available in their Shiny versions.

Dynamax will effectively add a whole new method of releasing Pokémon to the game - in the same way Shadow Pokémon did previously. Only new Pokémon caught from Max Battles, as well as their evolutions, will be able to Dynamax - pushing you to collect these new creatures as they're released.

items.title: New **Sonic the Hedgehog 3** film trailer reveals Keanu Reeves' Shadow
bullets: The release of Sonic the Hedgehog 3 on 20th December will round off Sega's Year of Shadow, which also includes Lego sets, a tour of the Shadow motorcycle, and the release in October of Sonic x Shadow Generations.

items.title: Two Wii games from the **MySims** series are getting re-released on Switch
bullets: MySims: Cozy Bundle offers MySims and MySims Kingdom for $39.99 / £34.99
Launches on Switch November 19

items.title: **Capcom Fighting Collection 2** features 8 games including Capcom vs SNK and Power Stone 1 & 2

bullets.text: Capcom vs SNK: Millennium Fight 2000 Pro
Capcom vs SNK 2: Mark of the Millennium 2001

bullets: The compilation will be released on Switch, PS4 and PC in 2025. As with other recent Capcom compilations, no Xbox version has been announced.
All eight games will feature online play with rollback netcode, as well as a training mode.
While announcing the new compilation, Capcom also confirmed that Marvel vs Capcom Fighting Collection: Arcade Classics will be released digitally on September 12 and physically on November 22

items.title: **Genshin Impact** Update 5.0 Launches Today
bullets: New Region; Natlan, and new characters added

items.title: **Digital Eclipse** is releasing a Tetris collection called **Tetris Forever**

bullets.text: An “accurate recreation”  of the original version on the Electronika 60 computer

bullets: Releases for "all major platforms" at some point in 2024

items.title: Square Enix’s **Foamstars** is officially going free to play in October
bullets: The game underperformed commercially, according to Square Enix
The Square Enix game, which was previously being sold on the PlayStation Store for $30 / £25, will be free to all users from October 4.
The game, which is still exclusive to Sony‘s consoles, was released earlier this year. The game was available to PlayStation Plus members at launch.

items.title: Service provider **PTW** has acquired co-development studio **Ghostpunch Games** for $13 million
bullets: The Florida-based studio has worked on notable titles including Risk of Rain 2, Fortnite, and Mortal Kombat 1
Notably, PTW believes the studio's vast experience can be combined with its own "deep QA knowledge" to offer new AI-based QA automation and other services to clients in the future.
